title: Shared Parental Leave (SPL)
text:
Shared Parental Leave (SPL) - SupportGoWhere
Shared Parental Leave (SPL)
Share link
Support eligible working parents in sharing parental responsibility and caring for their infants.


SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Government reimbursement/payment capped at $2,500 per week
Leave may be taken in one continuous block or non-continuously
Apply now
Collapse all sections
About this support




	

 SPL (2013)

	

SPL (from 1 Apr 2025)




Amount of leave entitled 

	

Eligible working fathers can take up to 4 weeks of leave from their wife’s Government-Paid Maternity Leave (GPML) or Adoption Leave (AL). 

	

Child’s birth or estimated delivery date or Formal Intent to Adopt (FIA) date: 

If between 1 Apr 2025 to 31 Mar 2026, up to 6 weeks of leave to be shared between parents 

If on or after 1 Apr 2026, up to 10 weeks of leave to be shared between parents 




Amount of government reimbursement/payment

	

Government reimbursement/payment capped at $2,500 per week, up to a total of $10,000 for 4 weeks. 

	

Government reimbursement/payment capped at $2,500 per week. 




Flexibility of leave 

	

Flexibility for leave to be taken in one continuous block or non-continuously, with mutual agreement between employer and employee. 




When can SPL be taken? 

	

Within 12 months from the child's date of birth. 

	

After Government-Paid Maternity Leave (GPML)/ Government-Paid Paternity Leave (GPPL)/ Adoption Leave (AL) is fully consumed; and  

Within 12 months from the child’s date of birth. 

Who is this for?

Parents whose child is born, or has an estimated delivery date (EDD), or formal intent to adopt (FIA) date on or after 1 Apr 2025, are entitled to the new SPL scheme and should not be making an election for 2013-SPL. 







	

SPL (2013) (For father) 

	

SPL (from 1 Apr 2025) (For both father and mother) 




For live births 

	

Your child is a Singapore citizen or becomes one within 12 months from birth  
 

Child's birth or estimated delivery date is between 1 Jul 2017 and 31 Mar 2025 (both dates inclusive) 
 

Wife is eligible for Government-Paid Maternity Leave or Adoption Leave and agrees to share the leave 
 

Married to child's mother between conception and birth or within 12 months from birth 

	

Your child is a Singapore citizen or becomes one within 12 months from birth  
 

Child’s birth or estimated delivery date:

If between 1 Apr 2025 to 31 Mar 2026, entitled to 6 weeks of SPL 

If on or after 1 Apr 2026, entitled to 10 weeks of SPL 

 

 For fathers: Lawfully married to the child’s mother between conception and birth or within 12 months from birth 
 

For employee: Served employer for at least 3 months before child’s birth  
 

For self-employed: Engaged in trade, business, profession, or vocation for at least 3 months before child’s birth 




For stillbirths 

	

The date of stillbirth is between 1 Nov 2021 and 31 Mar 2025 (both dates inclusive) 
 

Either you or the child’s mother are Singapore citizens 

	

NIL 




For adoptive child 

	

Formal Intent to Adopt (FIA) date between 1 Jul 2017 and 31 Mar 2025 (both dates inclusive) 
 

Married to the adoptive mother when the election for 2013-SPL was made 
 

If child is not Singapore citizen (SC), either you or the adoptive mother has been SC since the child’s dependant’s pass issuance 

	

Formal Intent to Adopt (FIA) date: 

If between 1 Apr 2025 to 31 Mar 2026, entitled to 6 weeks of SPL 

If on or after 1 Apr 2026, entitled to 10 weeks of SPL 




Not the natural father or mother of child 
 

If child is not Singapore citizen (SC), either adoptive mother or father of child has been SC since the child’s dependant’s pass issuance 
 

For employee: Served employer for at least 3 months before FIA date 
 

For self-employed: Engaged in trade, business, profession, or vocation for at least 3 months before FIA date 

How to apply?

For SPL (2013)
 

Discuss and agree on the leave arrangement with your wife. 

Your wife must declare her intention to share her leave through the Share My Leave eService in the Government-Paid Leave (GPL) Portal no earlier than 16 weeks before your child’s estimated delivery date. 




For employees: 

	

Notify your employer of the leave arrangement and submit the declaration form (SPL1) with necessary documents. 
 




For self-employed: 

	

Record your SPL dates and submit your claim online via the GPL Portal within 3 months after the last date of your SPL. 
 




For employers: 

	

Obtain and verify the declaration form (SPL1) and supporting documents from your employee. 

Keep the declaration form or records for 5 years from the last leave date for audit purposes. 

Verify that your employee’s wife has made a declaration to share her leave in the Government-Paid Leave (GPL) Portal. Check all supporting documents to confirm your employee’s eligibility. 

Submit your claim for reimbursement online via the GPL Portal no later than 3 months after the last date of your employee’s SPL. 




For SPL (from 1 Apr 2025) 

Please refer to details on the GPL Portal.




Where can I find help?

For SPL (from 1 Apr 2025), view more details. 

For the SPL (2013), view more details. 

MSFCares: 1800 111 2222

(say ‘GPLS’ when asked for the hotline you want to reach)

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Scheme last updated 15 Dec 2025
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
Answer a few questions to find schemes and services you may be eligible for.
